title: JavaScript Form Validation Quiz
description: Evaluate your understanding of form validation logic, built-in APIs, and core purposes in JavaScript.

questions.question: Which of the following describes the primary purpose of client-side JavaScript form validation?
options: To provide a secure way to prevent malicious input.
To enhance user experience by providing quick feedback and preventing unnecessary server requests.
To replace server-side validation completely.
To speed up database lookups.

questions.question: Which method in the Constraint Validation API returns true if an input contains valid data?
options: isValid()
testValidity()
checkValidity()
isCorrect()

questions.question: What does setCustomValidity(message) do?
options: It sets a custom CSS class for an input.
It sets a custom validation message to display to the user.
It sets a default value for an input.
It toggles the 'required' attribute on an input.

questions.question: In a form submission handler, how should you stop the form from being submitted to the server if validation fails?
options: return false; from the validation function
event.preventDefault(); within the submit handler
Both A and B.
None of the above.

questions.question: Can client-side validation be bypassed by malicious users (meaning it is not a security measure)?
options: Yes, easily.
No, it's a core security feature of browsers.
It depends on which browser is used.
Only if the user disables JavaScript.
